keyTerms.golden ratio: The irrational number approximately 1.618, found in nature and used in classical architecture for aesthetically pleasing proportions
keyTerms.harmonic proportions: Renaissance theory that the same mathematical ratios governing musical harmony should govern architectural space
keyTerms.Alberti: Leon Battista Alberti, fifteenth-century architect who codified the mathematical principles of Renaissance design
keyTerms.Palladian: The architectural style of Andrea Palladio, characterized by symmetry, classical temple fronts, and harmonic room proportions
keyTerms.neuroaesthetics: The scientific field studying how proportional relationships in space elicit consistent aesthetic preferences across cultures
